economic_notes: Fox Lake Cree Nation holds a 5% interest in Keeyask Hydro Power Limited Partnership valued at $59,354,167 cost. Multiple joint ventures with Sodexo Canada and York Factory First Nation for catering, janitorial, housekeeping, food and security services at Manitoba Hydro camps (Keewatinoow, Keeyask, Kettle). Fox and York Keeyask Joint Venture (50%) for employment retention and support services. A Kwis Mahka Joint Venture (50%) for decommissioning of Leewatinohk Camp. FLKII holds LP units in Keeyask Generating Station project. Fox Lake Hospitality operates gas bar, convenience store and restaurant on reserve. Fox Lake Gaming Centre and Smoke Shop generate discretionary revenue.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED on four bases: accounts receivable completeness/valuation, inventory existence/completeness, joint venture valuation, and accounts payable completeness. Fiscal year is 2021 not 2024. Large Manitoba Hydro loan of $68.45M (including $9.55M accrued interest) dominates long-term debt for Keeyask Hydro Power LP investment. Joint ventures with Sodexo and others not fully consolidated - accounted for by modified equity method. Ottawa Trust Fund included in restricted cash. Total government transfers estimated as ISC ($4,326,114) + FNIHB ($2,223,639) + CMHC ($139,818) + Province of Manitoba ($322,923) = $7,012,494. Net financial assets not explicitly stated on statement of financial position - statement not fully reproduced. Band housing ($1,727,534) and CMHC housing ($181,517) combined for expenses_housing. Audit report signed May 10, 2023 - over two years after fiscal year end, indicating significant delays.
